data.title: Logros y trayectoria
data.checkpoint_description: En esta unidad practicas el present perfect continuous (have/has been + -ing) para hablar de acciones que empezaron en el pasado y siguen ahora, con How long…? y los marcadores for y since. También trabajas el past perfect (had + participio) para una acción anterior a otro momento pasado, y lo contrastas con el pasado simple para ordenar bien los hechos en el tiempo.

lessons.title: Llevo tiempo haciéndolo
lessons.topic: Present perfect continuous (have/has been + -ing) para acciones que empezaron en el pasado y continúan

items.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción.

items.value: I've been working late lately.

items.prompt: Completa con present perfect continuous: "They ___ been training hard for the marathon."
items.text: They ___ been training hard for the marathon.
items.value: have
accepted: have
've

I've been looking for a new job
I have been looking for a new job.
I've been looking for a new job.

I've been searching for a new job
I have been searching for a new job.
I've been searching for a new job.

options: I've been meaning to call you all week.

items.value: I've been meaning to call you all week.

items.text: I've been thinking about my career a lot lately, and I feel ready for a change.

lessons.title: ¿Cuánto tiempo? for y since

accepted: for

data.title: Lo que dijeron
data.checkpoint_description: El estilo indirecto cuenta lo que alguien dijo sin repetirlo textualmente. Recuerda 'say' (sin objeto: she said that...) frente a 'tell' (con persona: she told me that...), el backshift de tiempos (is->was, will->would, can->could) y los cambios de pronombres. En preguntas indirectas se usa 'asked if/whether' o 'asked what/where' con orden de afirmación (sin do/did), y para órdenes y peticiones 'told/asked somebody to do'. Las expresiones de tiempo y lugar cambian: today->that day, yesterday->the day before, here->there.

lessons.title: Dijo que…: say vs tell y backshift
lessons.topic: Reported statements: say vs tell and tense backshift

items.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción.
Completa: "She ___ me that she was tired."

items.prompt: Elige el estilo indirecto correcto de "I am busy" (he said).
items.value: He said he was busy.

items.prompt: Completa con el backshift de "works": "He said he ___ in a bank."
items.text: He said he ___ in a bank.
items.value: worked
accepted: worked

items.prompt: Traduce: "Me dijo que estaba cansada."
items.source: Me dijo que estaba cansada.
items.value: She told me she was tired.
accepted: She told me she was tired.
She told me she was tired
She told me that she was tired.
She told me that she was tired
He told me he was tired.
He told me he was tired

Escucha y elige lo que oíste.
options: He told me he had already finished the report.
He said he would finish the report tomorrow.
He asked me to finish the report today.
items.value: He told me he had already finished the report.

items.text: She said she didn't have time, but she told me she would call later.

data.title: Cómo se hace y las noticias
data.checkpoint_description: En esta unidad dominas la voz pasiva: "be" + participio para hablar de procesos y noticias sin nombrar quién actúa. La usas en presente (The coffee is grown in Brazil) y en pasado (The bridge was built in 1920), añades el agente con "by" cuando importa (The film was directed by a Spaniard) y aprendes el causativo "have something done" para decir que mandas hacer algo (I had my car repaired).

lessons.title: Se hace así: la pasiva en presente
lessons.topic: Voz pasiva en presente simple (am/is/are + participio) para describir procesos y hechos

items.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción.

is grown.

Bread is made fresh every morning
Bread is made fresh every morning.

data.title: Hipótesis y arrepentimientos
data.checkpoint_description: En esta unidad dominas el tercer condicional (If + past perfect, would have + participio) para hablar de situaciones pasadas irreales y sus consecuencias hipotéticas. Practicas los condicionales mixtos, que conectan una causa pasada con un resultado presente, y usas I wish / if only + past perfect para expresar arrepentimientos sobre el pasado. Al final repasas los tres condicionales (1.º, 2.º y 3.º) en contexto para elegir el correcto según la situación.

lessons.title: Si lo hubiera sabido: tercer condicional
lessons.topic: Tercer condicional para situaciones pasadas irreales: If + past perfect, would have + participio

items.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción.

If I had known, I would have helped you.

items.value: If I had known, I would have called you.

items.prompt: Lee en voz alta:

data.title: Personas, lugares y definiciones
data.checkpoint_description: En esta unidad dominas las oraciones de relativo. Usas las especificativas (defining), sin comas, para identificar a quién o a qué te refieres con who (personas), which/that (cosas), whose (posesión) y where (lugares), y construyes definiciones del tipo the person who… / the place where… Distingues las explicativas (non-defining), que van entre comas y solo añaden información extra (sin that). Por último, aprendes a omitir el relativo cuando funciona como objeto del verbo (the film I saw), algo imposible cuando es sujeto.

lessons.title: El que y la que: definir personas y cosas
lessons.topic: Relativas especificativas con the one(s) who/that para señalar exactamente a quién o a qué te refieres.

items.prompt: Empareja cada expresión con su traducción.
Completa con el relativo de posesión: "She's the colleague ___ presentation impressed the whole team."
items.value: whose

items.prompt: Elige la frase correcta para "Esa es la cafetería que recomienda todo el mundo".
options: That's the café that everyone recommends.
That's the café who everyone recommends.
That's the café everyone recommends it.
items.value: That's the café that everyone recommends.

items.prompt: Completa con el relativo para personas: "He's the one ___ called you yesterday."
items.text: He's the one ___ called you yesterday.

that

Escucha y elige lo que oíste.
options: He's the one who fixed my computer last week.

items.value: He's the one who fixed my computer last week.

items.text: The teacher who helped me the most is the one I still remember.

lessons.title: Who, which, that, whose, where
lessons.topic: Elegir el relativo correcto según describas personas, cosas, posesión o lugares en cláusulas especificativas.

items.prompt: Empareja cada relativo con su uso.
Completa: "That's the woman ___ car was stolen last night."
items.value: whose

items.prompt: Completa: "This is the hotel ___ we stayed two summers ago."
items.value: where

items.prompt: Completa con el relativo para cosas: "The report ___ arrived this morning is full of mistakes."
items.text: The report ___ arrived this morning is full of mistakes.
items.value: which
accepted: which
that

items.prompt: Traduce: "Conozco a un hombre cuyo hermano trabaja en la empresa."

lessons.title: Entre comas: información extra
lessons.topic: Relativas explicativas (non-defining), entre comas, que solo añaden datos adicionales y nunca usan that.

items.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción.
Completa la explicativa: "My boss, ___ has worked here for years, is retiring soon."
items.value: who

items.prompt: Elige la frase explicativa correcta para "Berlín, que visité el año pasado, es preciosa".
options: Berlin, which I visited last year, is beautiful.
Berlin, that I visited last year, is beautiful.
Berlin, who I visited last year, is beautiful.
items.value: Berlin, which I visited last year, is beautiful.

items.prompt: Completa la explicativa con el relativo para cosas: "Our new office, ___ opened in May, is much bigger."
items.text: Our new office, ___ opened in May, is much bigger.
items.value: which
accepted: which

accepted: My sister, who lives in Madrid, is coming this weekend.

My sister, who lives in Madrid, comes this weekend.

lessons.title: Cuándo se puede omitir el relativo
lessons.topic: Omisión del pronombre relativo cuando es objeto del verbo, e imposibilidad de omitirlo cuando es sujeto.

items.prompt: Empareja cada palabra con su traducción.
¿En qué frase se puede OMITIR el relativo? Elige esa frase.
options: The film we watched last night was amazing.
The man who called you left a message.
The bus that goes downtown is late.
items.value: The film we watched last night was amazing.

items.prompt: Elige la frase correcta sin pronombre relativo para "El libro que me prestaste es genial".
options: The book you lent me is great.
The book who you lent me is great.
The book lent me you is great.
items.value: The book you lent me is great.

data.title: Deducciones y debate
data.checkpoint_description: En esta unidad deduces sobre el pasado con modales perfectos: 'must have' (seguridad), 'might/could have' (posibilidad) y 'can't have' (imposibilidad), siempre seguidos de participio. También expresas crítica y arrepentimiento con 'should have' y 'shouldn't have', y la idea de un esfuerzo innecesario con 'needn't have'. Por último, practicas phrasal verbs y colocaciones de uso frecuente y el lenguaje para opinar y argumentar (in my opinion, on the other hand, however).

lessons.title: Debe de haber…: deducción sobre el pasado
lessons.topic: Deducir lo que pasó con must / might / could / can't have + participio

items.prompt: Empareja cada expresión con su traducción.
Completa (deducción casi segura): "The ground is wet, so it ___ rained last night."
items.value: must have

items.prompt: Completa (algo imposible): "She ___ seen me; I wasn't even there."
items.value: can't have

items.prompt: Completa con deducción de posibilidad (might/may/could + participio): "He isn't answering. He ___ left already."
items.text: He isn't answering. He ___ left already.
items.value: might have
accepted: might have
may have
could have
might've
may've
could've

items.prompt: Traduce: "Debe de haber olvidado la reunión."
items.source: Debe de haber olvidado la reunión.
items.value: He must have forgotten the meeting
accepted: He must have forgotten the meeting
He must have forgotten the meeting.
She must have forgotten the meeting
She must have forgotten the meeting.
They must have forgotten the meeting
They must have forgotten the meeting.
He must've forgotten the meeting
She must've forgotten the meeting
They must've forgotten the meeting

items.prompt: Arma la frase: "No pueden haber terminado tan rápido".
Escucha y elige lo que oíste.
options: She must have missed the train.
She might have missed the bus.
She can't have caught the train.
items.value: She must have missed the train.

items.prompt: Lee en voz alta:
items.text: They must have left early because the lights are off.

lessons.title: should have / needn't have: crítica y arrepentimiento
lessons.topic: Criticar el pasado y expresar arrepentimiento o esfuerzo innecesario

items.prompt: Empareja cada expresión con su traducción.
Completa (reproche por algo no hecho): "You ___ told me earlier; I would have helped."
items.value: should have

items.prompt: Completa (esfuerzo innecesario): "You ___ cooked so much; we hardly ate anything."
items.value: needn't have

items.prompt: Completa con crítica negativa (shouldn't + participio): "I ___ said that; it was rude."
items.text: I ___ said that; it was rude.
items.value: shouldn't have
accepted: shouldn't have
should not have

items.prompt: Traduce: "Deberías haber pedido ayuda."
items.source: Deberías haber pedido ayuda.
items.value: You should have asked for help
accepted: You should have asked for help
You should have asked for help.
You should've asked for help
You should've asked for help.
You should have asked for help!
You should have asked for some help
You should have asked for some help.
You should've asked for some help
You should've asked for some help.

items.prompt: Ordena: "No deberíamos haber esperado tanto".
items.value: We shouldn't have waited so long

items.prompt: Escucha y elige lo que oíste.
options: You needn't have worried about it.
You shouldn't have worried about it.
You must have worried about it.
items.value: You needn't have worried about it.

items.prompt: Lee en voz alta:
items.text: I should have booked the tickets sooner, honestly.

lessons.title: Phrasal verbs y colocaciones frecuentes
lessons.topic: Phrasal verbs comunes y colocaciones útiles para el debate

items.prompt: Empareja cada phrasal verb con su traducción.
Completa: "Can you ___ a better solution by tomorrow?"
items.value: come up with

items.prompt: Elige la colocación correcta: "She ___ a strong argument during the meeting."
items.value: made

items.prompt: Completa el phrasal verb (averiguar): "It took me a while to ___ out how this machine operates."
items.text: It took me a while to ___ out how this machine operates.
items.value: figure
accepted: figure
work

items.prompt: Traduce: "Quiero sacar un tema importante."
items.source: Quiero sacar un tema importante.
items.value: I want to bring up an important point
accepted: I want to bring up an important point
I want to bring up an important point.
I want to bring up an important topic
I want to bring up an important topic.
I'd like to bring up an important point
I'd like to bring up an important point.
I want to raise an important point
I want to raise an important point.

items.prompt: Arma la frase: "Necesito señalar un par de problemas".
Escucha y elige lo que oíste.
options: We need to figure out what went wrong.
We need to point out what went wrong.
We need to bring up what went wrong.
items.value: We need to figure out what went wrong.

items.prompt: Lee en voz alta:

lessons.title: En mi opinión / por otro lado: argumentar
lessons.topic: Expresar opiniones, contrastar ideas y estructurar un argumento

items.prompt: Empareja cada conector con su traducción.
Elige el conector de contraste: "The plan is cheap; ___, it's quite risky."
items.value: however

items.prompt: Completa la expresión de opinión: "___ my opinion, remote work is more productive."
items.value: In

items.prompt: Completa con la expresión de contraste (por otro lado): "It saves money. On the other ___, it takes longer."
items.text: It saves money. On the other ___, it takes longer.
items.value: hand
accepted: hand

items.prompt: Traduce: "En mi opinión, deberíamos considerar ambos puntos de vista."
items.source: En mi opinión, deberíamos considerar ambos puntos de vista.
items.value: In my opinion, we should consider both points of view
accepted: In my opinion, we should consider both points of view
In my opinion, we should consider both points of view.
In my opinion we should consider both points of view
In my opinion, we should consider both viewpoints
In my opinion, we should consider both viewpoints.
In my view, we should consider both points of view
In my view, we should consider both points of view.

items.prompt: Ordena (empieza por "Although"): "Aunque veo tu punto, no estoy de acuerdo".
items.value: Although I see your point I disagree

items.prompt: Escucha y elige lo que oíste.
options: On the other hand, it could backfire.
On the one hand, it could work well.
In other words, it might backfire.
items.value: On the other hand, it could backfire.

items.prompt: Lee en voz alta:
items.text: I see your point, but I think there's a stronger argument for waiting.
